channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Actions in Horn of Africa Raise Questions on Sudan Policy Under Trump
item.description: The Donald Trump administration has taken several steps that worry many people about the United States strategy in Sudan and the Horn of Africa. Although the US claims it wants to act as an honest bro...

item.title: House Democrats investigate alleged misuse of America250 celebration funding and governance
item.description: House Democrats have launched scrutiny into allegations surrounding the organization of America’s upcoming 250th Independence Day celebration, claiming that the White House attempted to exert undue po...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over AI-Generated Influencers Promoting New Gay Dating App Goose
item.description: A recent WIRED report examines controversy surrounding Goose, an invite-only dating and social app aimed at gay men that markets itself as an alternative to hookup-focused platforms like Grindr. The a...

item.title: NBC boosts investment in Tanzanian football with expanded youth and player development programmes
item.description: The National Bank of Commerce (NBC) has confirmed its continued and expanded commitment to developing football in Tanzania through its sponsorship of the NBC Premier League. The bank announced new ini...

item.title: US-Iran conflict disrupts LNG supply chain while Shell projects long-term global demand growth
item.description: The report explains how tensions from the US-Iran war have seriously disrupted global liquefied natural gas (LNG) trade, creating shocks across energy markets and shipping routes. According to Shell’s...

item.title: Go Outdoors discount codes and saving tips for July 2026
item.description: This article from The Independent provides a detailed overview of current Go Outdoors discount codes and money-saving opportunities available in July 2026. It focuses on how shoppers can reduce the co...

item.title: Golden Cape Woven from Silk of Over One Million Spiders Created in Madagascar Over Eight Years
item.description: A remarkable golden cape, woven entirely from the natural silk of more than one million Golden Orb-Weaver spiders, has captured attention for its extraordinary craftsmanship and the dedication behind ...

item.title: Norfolk Naval Shipyard Paint Shop (Shop 71) Highlights Critical Corrosion Control and Ship Preservation Work
item.description: The article highlights the essential work performed by the Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY) Paint Shop, known as Shop 71, which plays a vital role in preserving and protecting U.S. Navy vessels. Far beyo...

item.title: YSRCP to Project MAVIGUN as Alternative Development Model for Andhra Pradesh in 2029 Polls
item.description: YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) president and former Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Y.S. Jagan Mohan Reddy has announced that the Machilipatnam-Vijayawada-Guntur (MAVIGUN) proposal will be the party’s centr...

item.title: Rappler Investigates: Questions Raised Over P75M Donations and Plunder Complaint Against Senator Marcoleta
item.description: This Rappler Investigates newsletter examines the controversy surrounding Senator Rodante Marcoleta’s alleged undeclared campaign funds and the subsequent plunder complaint filed against him. The repo...

item.title: Child dies and four injured after roof collapse at under-construction house in Lahore
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Lahore’s Baghbanpura neighbourhood where the roof of an under-construction house collapsed, resulting in the death of a child and injuries to at least four other people. ...

item.title: Holgado and Joan Mir set to join Gresini MotoGP line-up from 2027
item.description: Gresini Racing has confirmed a significant reshuffle of its MotoGP rider line-up for the 2027 season, announcing the arrival of rising Spanish talent Daniel Holgado alongside former world champion Joa...

item.title: Central Bank of Nigeria Revokes Operating Licences of 46 Microfinance Banks Over Regulatory Failures
item.description: My people, the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) under Governor Olayemi Cardoso has taken strong action by revoking the operating licences of 46 microfinance banks across the country with effect from July...

item.title: Federal High Court introduces weekend filing system for pre-election cases ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The Federal High Court (FHC) in Nigeria has introduced a new Practice Direction that allows the filing of pre-election cases on weekends and public holidays as part of efforts to fast-track electoral ...

item.title: Concerns Over Withheld Voting Machine Security Report and Potential Election Interference in 2026 Midterms
item.description: In this opinion piece, progressive commentator Thom Hartmann discusses a federal intelligence report on voting machine security that the White House has allegedly withheld ahead of the 2026 midterms. ...

item.title: Jen Psaki Points to Timing of Trump's Stock Purchases Around Tariff Policy Announcements
item.description: In a recent segment on MS NOW, former White House Press Secretary Jen Psaki discussed revelations from President Donald Trump's extensive financial disclosure documents. Psaki highlighted stock trades...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif to undertake official visits to Iran and Turkiye from July 3–5
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is scheduled to visit Iran and Turkiye from July 3 to July 5, as confirmed by the Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi during a weekly media briefing. The visit will...

item.title: Iowa GOP lieutenant governor candidate denies link to Adult Friend Finder email account after data breach report
item.description: A married Iowa Republican running for lieutenant governor has denied any involvement with an alleged Adult Friend Finder account tied to his email address following a reported data breach disclosure. ...

item.title: Norovirus outbreak affects over 120 passengers on Princess Cruises ship Ruby Princess
item.description: More than 120 people became ill aboard the Princess Cruises ship Ruby Princess during a round-trip voyage from San Francisco to Alaska and Canada, according to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and...
